description_long: This is a hanging potted plant featuring a weathered, grayish pot suspended by three thin, tan cords that converge at a loop for hanging. The pot is filled with dark brown soil and supports a trailing vine plant with heart-shaped, medium to dark green leaves, some of which have lighter undersides. The plant's stems drape over the edge of the pot, creating a cascading effect. The pot itself has a slightly rough, marbled texture and appears to be made of concrete or a similar material. There are no visible decorative patterns or labels on the pot, and the overall design is utilitarian and understated. This type of plant is commonly used to add greenery to urban environments, especially in areas where floor space is limited.
description: A hanging potted plant with trailing green vines in a weathered gray pot suspended by tan cords. The pot has a rough, marbled texture and no visible decoration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pot's marbled texture, tan hanging cords, and cascading green leaves spilling over the edge.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the dense foliage and the way the vines drape more heavily on one side of the pot.
description_view_2: Angled view displaying the trailing stems and the pot's rounded shape, with leaves extending outward and downward.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the pot and the soil surface, with fewer visible leaves and a clearer view of the hanging cords.
